manifest.title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard v4.0

controls.title: Prohibition of Sensitive Authentication Data Storage
controls.clause_text: Sensitive authentication data (SAD) is not stored after authorization.
controls.requirement: Organizations must not store sensitive authentication data after the authorization process is complete

controls.title: Restrict Access to Full PAN Displays and Copy Functions
controls.clause_text: Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy PAN are restricted.
controls.requirement: Organizations must implement restrictions on access to displays showing full primary account numbers and prevent unauthorized copying of PAN data.

controls.title: Primary Account Number Security in Storage
controls.clause_text: Primary account number (PAN) is secured wherever it is stored.
controls.requirement: Organizations must implement security controls to protect primary account numbers in all storage locations and systems where they are maintained.

controls.title: Sensitive Authentication Data Not Stored After Authorization
controls.clause_text: SAD is not stored after authorization, even if encrypted. All sensitive authentication data received is rendered unrecoverable upon completion of the authorization process.
controls.requirement: Organizations must not store sensitive authentication data after authorization is complete and must render any received sensitive authentication data unrecoverable upon completion of the authorization process.

controls.title: Full Track Data Not Stored After Authorization
controls.clause_text: The full contents of any track are not stored upon completion of the authorization process.
controls.requirement: Organizations must not store the full contents of any track data from payment cards upon completion of the authorization process.

controls.title: Card Verification Code Not Stored After Authorization
controls.clause_text: The card verification code is not stored upon completion of the authorization process.
controls.requirement: Organizations must not store card verification codes after the authorization process is complete.

controls.title: PIN and PIN Block Not Stored After Authorization
controls.clause_text: The personal identification number (PIN) and the PIN block are not stored upon completion of the authorization process.
controls.requirement: Organizations must not store personal identification numbers (PINs) and PIN blocks after the authorization process is complete.

controls.title: Encryption of SAD stored before authorization completion
controls.clause_text: SAD that is stored electronically prior to completion of authorization is encrypted using strong cryptography.
controls.requirement: Sensitive authentication data that is stored electronically before authorization is completed must be encrypted using strong cryptographic methods.

controls.title: PAN Masking When Displayed
controls.clause_text: PAN is masked when displayed (the BIN and last four digits are the maximum number of digits to be displayed), such that only personnel with a legitimate business need can see more than the BIN and last four digits of the PAN.
controls.requirement: Primary Account Numbers must be masked during display, showing only the Bank Identification Number and last four digits, with full PAN access restricted to personnel with documented legitimate business need.

controls.title: PAN Protection with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
controls.clause_text: PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission
controls.requirement: Primary account numbers (PAN) must be protected using strong cryptographic methods when transmitted over networks

controls.title: Wireless Network Cryptography
controls.clause_text: 4.2.1.2 Wireless networks transmitting PAN or connected to the CDE use industry best practices to implement strong cryptography for authentication and transmission.
controls.requirement: Wireless networks that transmit PAN or connect to the cardholder data environment must implement strong cryptography following industry best practices for both authentication and data transmission to prevent unauthorized access and eavesdropping.

controls.title: PAN Protection with Strong Cryptography in End-User Messaging
controls.clause_text: PAN is secured with strong cryptography whenever it is sent via end-user messaging technologies.
controls.requirement: Organizations must implement strong cryptographic protection for Primary Account Numbers (PAN) when transmitted through end-user messaging technologies such as email, instant messaging, or chat applications.

controls.title: Secure Development of Bespoke and Custom Software
controls.clause_text: Bespoke and custom software are developed securely.
controls.requirement: Organizations must ensure that any custom or bespoke software they develop follows secure development practices

controls.title: Software Development Personnel Security Training
controls.clause_text:
Software development personnel working on bespoke and custom software are trained at least once every 12 months as follows:
• On software security relevant to their job function and development languages.
• Including secure software design and secure coding techniques.
• Including, if security testing tools are used, how to use the tools for detecting vulnerabilities in software.
controls.requirement: Software development personnel must receive annual training on software security relevant to their job function, secure design and coding techniques, and security testing tools if applicable

controls.title: Code Review for Bespoke and Custom Software
controls.clause_text: Bespoke and custom software is reviewed prior to being released into production or to customers, to identify and correct potential coding vulnerabilities, as follows: • Code reviews ensure code is developed according to secure coding guidelines. • Code reviews look for both existing and emerging software vulnerabilities. • Appropriate corrections are implemented prior to release.
controls.requirement: All bespoke and custom software must undergo code reviews before production release to identify vulnerabilities, ensure secure coding guidelines compliance, and implement necessary corrections

controls.title: Manual Code Review Requirements
controls.clause_text: If manual code reviews are performed for bespoke and custom software prior to release to production, code changes are: • Reviewed by individuals other than the originating code author, and who are knowledgeable about code-review techniques and secure coding practices. • Reviewed and approved by management prior to release.
controls.requirement: Manual code reviews must be conducted by qualified individuals other than the original author and require management approval before release to production

controls.title: Software Engineering Techniques for Attack Prevention
controls.clause_text: Examine documented procedures and interview responsible software development personnel to verify that software engineering techniques or other methods are defined and in use by developers of bespoke and custom software to prevent or mitigate all common software attacks as specified in this requirement.
controls.requirement: Organizations must define and implement software engineering techniques or other methods for developers of bespoke and custom software to prevent or mitigate common software attacks

controls.title: Strong Authentication Management
controls.clause_text: Strong authentication for users and administrators is established and managed.
controls.requirement: Organizations must establish and manage strong authentication mechanisms for both regular users and administrators

controls.title: Strong cryptography for authentication factors
controls.clause_text: Strong cryptography is used to render all authentication factors unreadable during transmission and storage on all system components.
controls.requirement: All authentication factors must be protected using strong cryptography to make them unreadable when transmitted or stored on any system component.

controls.title: User Identity Verification Before Authentication Factor Modification
controls.clause_text: User identity is verified before modifying any authentication factor.
controls.requirement: Organizations must verify a user's identity before allowing any changes to authentication factors such as passwords, tokens, or biometric data.

controls.title: Password History Restriction
controls.clause_text: Individuals are not allowed to submit a new password/passphrase that is the same as any of the last four passwords/passphrases used.
controls.requirement: Users must not be able to reuse any of their last four passwords or passphrases when creating a new one

controls.title: Authentication Policies Documentation and Communication
controls.clause_text: Authentication policies and procedures are documented and communicated to all users including: • Guidance on selecting strong authentication factors. • Guidance for how users should protect their authentication factors. • Instructions not to reuse previously used passwords/passphrases. • Instructions to change passwords/passphrases if there is any suspicion or knowledge that the password/passphrases have been compromised and how to report the incident.
controls.requirement: Organizations must document and communicate authentication policies to all users, including guidance on selecting and protecting authentication factors, instructions against password reuse, and procedures for changing compromised passwords and reporting incidents.

controls.title: Password/Passphrase Management for Single-Factor Authentication
controls.clause_text: If passwords/passphrases are used as the only authentication factor for user access, inspect system configuration settings to verify that passwords/passphrases are managed in accordance with ONE of the elements specified in this requirement.
controls.requirement: When passwords or passphrases are the sole authentication method, they must be managed according to specified security elements to prevent indefinite use of compromised credentials

controls.title: Audit logs for anomaly detection and forensic analysis
controls.clause_text: Audit logs are implemented to support the detection of anomalies and suspicious activity, and the forensic analysis of events.
controls.requirement: Organizations must implement audit logs that can detect anomalies and suspicious activity and support forensic analysis of security events.

controls.title: Audit Logs Enabled for All System Components
controls.clause_text: Audit logs are enabled and active for all system components and cardholder data.
controls.requirement: Organizations must ensure that audit logging is enabled and actively running on all system components that process or store cardholder data.

controls.title: Audit Logging of Individual User Access to Cardholder Data
controls.clause_text: 10.2.1.1 Audit logs capture all individual user access to cardholder data.
controls.requirement: Organizations must configure audit logs to capture and record all instances of individual user access to cardholder data

controls.title: Audit Logging of Administrative Access Actions
controls.clause_text: 10.2.1.2 Audit logs capture all actions taken by any individual with administrative access, including any interactive use of application or system accounts.
controls.requirement: Organizations must configure audit logs to capture all actions performed by individuals with administrative access, including interactive use of application or system accounts

controls.title: Audit Logging of Audit Log Access
controls.clause_text: 10.2.1.3 Audit logs capture all access to audit logs.
controls.requirement: Organizations must configure audit logs to capture all access attempts to the audit logs themselves

controls.title: Audit Logging of Invalid Access Attempts
controls.clause_text: 10.2.1.4 Audit logs capture all invalid logical access attempts.
controls.requirement: Organizations must configure audit logs to capture all invalid logical access attempts to systems

controls.title: Audit Logging of Authentication Credential Changes
controls.clause_text: 10.2.1.5 Audit logs capture all changes to identification and authentication credentials including, but not limited to: • Creation of new accounts. • Elevation of privileges. • All changes, additions, or deletions to accounts with administrative access.
controls.requirement: Organizations must configure audit logs to capture all changes to identification and authentication credentials, including account creation, privilege elevation, and administrative account modifications

controls.title: Audit Logging of Audit Log Status Changes
controls.clause_text: 10.2.1.6 Audit logs capture the following: • All initialization of new audit logs, and • All starting, stopping, or pausing of the existing audit logs.
controls.requirement: Organizations must configure audit logs to capture initialization of new audit logs and any starting, stopping, or pausing of existing audit logs

controls.title: Audit Logging of System-Level Object Changes
controls.clause_text: 10.2.1.7 Audit logs capture all creation and deletion of system-level objects.
controls.requirement: Organizations must configure audit logs to capture all creation and deletion of system-level objects

controls.title: Verify audit log entries contain all required elements for auditable events
controls.clause_text: Interview personnel and examine audit log configurations and log data to verify that all elements specified in this requirement are included in log entries for each auditable event (from 10.2.1.1 through 10.2.1.7).
controls.requirement: Organizations must ensure that audit log entries contain all specified data elements for each type of auditable event defined in requirements 10.2.1.1 through 10.2.1.7

controls.title: PAN is rendered unreadable anywhere it is stored
controls.clause_text: PAN is rendered unreadable anywhere it is stored by using any of the following approaches: One-way hashes based on strong cryptography of the entire PAN. Truncation. Index tokens. Strong cryptography with associated key-management processes and procedures.
controls.requirement: Tokenize PAN with a PCI-validated provider; store the token only.

controls.title: Strong cryptography for PAN in transit over open, public networks
controls.clause_text: PAN is secured with strong cryptography whenever it is sent via end-user messaging technologies. Strong cryptography is used to safeguard PAN during transmission over open, public networks.
controls.requirement: Use HTTPS (TLS 1.2+) for all PAN transmission.

controls.title: Passwords/passphrases meet minimum complexity requirements
controls.clause_text: If passwords/passphrases are used as an authentication factor, they meet the following minimum level of complexity: A minimum length of 12 characters.
controls.requirement: Set minimum password length to 12 or greater.

spec.question: Does this SECURITY.md credibly describe a SAST-in-CI process satisfying PCI-DSS 6.2.4? Reply with one word: PASS or UNCERTAIN.
spec.remediation: Expand SECURITY.md to describe SAST tooling, frequency, and CI gating.
